pi_items.source: Cannot verify without direct access to Ekylibre demo database schema and demo product data. However, quantity of 25 is a reasonable order magnitude for grain/cereal sales.

Comparable to France agricultural grain prices (2024-2025). EU cereals prices in this period ranged approximately 200-220 EUR per metric tonne. Price is plausible but unit of measurement (per kg? per tonne? per quintal?) is not specified in task.json, preventing definitive verification.
Verification logic in verifier.py (line 107-109) confirms this tolerance is used for quantity validation. Tolerance value matches verification implementation.
Verification logic in verifier.py (line 117-119) confirms this tolerance is used for price validation. Tolerance value matches verification implementation.

pi_items.verified_value: Confirmed real: GAEC JOULIN farm dataset is publicly available at https://github.com/ekylibre/first_run-demo, sourced from an actual French farm in Charente-Maritime (17250 SAINT-PORCHAIRE). Used by Ekylibre developers for demonstration and testing.
pi_items.source: GitHub repository https://github.com/ekylibre/first_run-demo and setup_ekylibre.sh script (lines 112-116) confirm cloning of real farm demo data

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real farm data from GAEC JOULIN, a documented French agricultural farm located in Charente-Maritime. The expected quantity (25) and unit pretax price (210.00 EUR) specified in metadata are plausible for grain/cereal products in the French market based on 2024-2025 agricultural commodity prices (200-220 EUR/tonne range). However, without access to the actual Ekylibre demo database schema and specific product definitions, the exact units and whether these precise values exist in the demo dataset cannot be independently confirmed. The verification tolerances (±0.5 for quantity, ±1.0 for price) are correctly implemented in the verifier.py logic.
